skill.name: docx-feedback-tracker
skill.description: Automatically detect DOCX track-changes history, version rounds, and author feedback to generate a Markdown modification explanation file when you run it on one or more DOCX versions.
skill.summary: Extract tracked changes, author feedback, and version diffs from DOCX files into structured Markdown reports.
skill.author: AIPOCH

security_audit.summary: All 66 static analysis findings are false positives. The 'weak cryptographic algorithm' findings flagged YAML front matter, argument parser setup, and documentation text that contain no cryptographic operations. The 'Ruby/shell backtick execution' findings flagged Markdown code formatting backticks used in reference documentation and SKILL.md code blocks. The 'hardcoded URL' findings flagged an XML namespace constant used for DOCX parsing and a GitHub source citation URL. The 'hard link creation' findings flagged Python string splitlines() and list filtering. The 'obfuscation' finding flagged legitimate Python f-string formatting for XML attribute access. The zipfile import is legitimate because DOCX files are ZIP archives. No actual security threats or malicious code patterns were identified.

content.user_title: Track DOCX Revisions and Generate Change Logs
content.value_statement: Academic editors and document reviewers spend hours manually comparing DOCX versions to identify changes. This skill reads tracked changes, comments, and version diffs from DOCX files and outputs a consolidated Markdown report organized by author and revision round.
actual_capabilities: Extract insertions and deletions from DOCX track-changes using w:ins and w:del XML tags
Extract author-attributed comments from DOCX comments.xml files
Group multiple DOCX versions into round-based change logs with version ordering
Fall back to text diffs when track changes are not present in the document
Generate consolidated Markdown output files saved next to the source DOCX files
Support batch processing of entire directories with glob pattern matching
limitations: Requires Python 3.x and the docx2python library to be installed locally
Cannot attribute author names when using the text-diff fallback mode
Only processes DOCX files and does not support older DOC format
Text diff mode may produce false positives from link tag removal and whitespace normalization

use_cases.title: Academic Peer Review Tracking
use_cases.description: An academic researcher receives a revised manuscript with tracked changes from a journal editor and needs a clean summary of all modifications organized by reviewer.

use_cases.title: Editorial Team Revision Consolidation
use_cases.description: An editorial team works on a document through multiple revision rounds and needs a consolidated changelog showing who changed what in each round.

use_cases.title: Compliance Document Audit
use_cases.description: A compliance officer needs to audit document revisions across multiple versions of a policy document to verify all required changes were made.

prompt_templates.title: Single DOCX Review
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the docx-feedback-tracker skill to analyze this DOCX file and generate a change log. The file is at path/to/document.docx.
prompt_templates.scenario: User has one DOCX file with tracked changes enabled

prompt_templates.title: Multiple Version Comparison
prompt_templates.prompt: Use docx-feedback-tracker to compare these DOCX versions and create a round-by-round change log. Files: report-v1.docx, report-v2.docx, report-v3.docx.
prompt_templates.scenario: User has multiple versioned DOCX files to compare sequentially

prompt_templates.title: Batch Directory Processing
prompt_templates.prompt: Use docx-feedback-tracker to process all DOCX files in the reviews/ directory. Group files by topic series and generate one change log per series.
prompt_templates.scenario: User needs to batch process a folder of DOCX files grouped by topic

prompt_templates.title: Custom Template Integration
prompt_templates.prompt: Use docx-feedback-tracker to analyze these DOCX files and integrate the change log output with the template at assets/change-log-template.md for custom formatting.
prompt_templates.scenario: User wants to customize the output format using the provided template

output_examples.input: A single DOCX file named manuscript-v2.docx containing tracked changes from two reviewers.
output_examples.output: A Markdown file named manuscript-change-log.md with sections showing inserted text, deleted text, and comments grouped by author for version v2.

output_examples.input: Three DOCX files named draft-v1.docx, draft-v2.docx, and draft-v3.docx representing three revision rounds.
output_examples.output: A consolidated Markdown file named draft-change-log.md with three round sections, each showing author changes, new content, removed content, and pending comments for that version.

output_examples.input: A DOCX file with no tracked changes and a second file representing the next version.
output_examples.output: A Markdown file showing text differences between versions with a note that changes cannot be attributed to specific authors.

best_practices: Enable track changes in your word processor before editing documents to preserve full author attribution.
Use consistent version suffixes like -v1, -v2, -v3 in file names to ensure correct round ordering.
Keep all versions of a document series in the same directory so the skill can group them correctly.
anti_patterns: Manually opening each DOCX version to compare changes by eye instead of using the automated change log.
Running the script on a single file with no track changes and expecting author attribution in the output.
Placing unrelated documents in the same directory expecting the skill to separate them by content topics.

faq.question: What does the DOCX feedback tracker do?
faq.answer: It reads DOCX files and generates a Markdown report of all tracked changes, comments, and version differences organized by author and revision round.

faq.question: Do I need to install additional software to use this skill?
faq.answer: Yes, you need Python 3.x and the docx2python library installed on your system to execute the script.

faq.question: Can it process multiple DOCX files at the same time?
faq.answer: Yes, you can pass multiple files as command arguments or use the --dir option to process an entire directory.

faq.question: Does it work with documents that have no track changes?
faq.answer: Yes, it falls back to a text diff between versions, but changes cannot be attributed to specific authors in this mode.

faq.question: What format is the output file?
faq.answer: The output is a Markdown file with sections for each version round, listing inserted content, deleted content, and author comments.

faq.question: Can the skill handle comments from multiple authors?
faq.answer: Yes, it extracts and groups comments by author name from the DOCX comments.xml file and shows counts per author.
